title: Mouthwatering Yeasty Irish Flour Scones
description: These scones are a classic Irish dish featuring strong white flour and render-fat, making them a crowd-pleaser, and are also ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, yeast, salt, and sugar.
Add the rendered fat and use a pastry blender or fingers to work it into the dry ingredients until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Make a well in the center of the mixture and add the warm water, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together in a shaggy mass.
Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and knead for 5-7 minutes, until the dough becomes smooth and elastic.
Pat the dough into a circle that is about 1 inch thick.
Use a biscuit cutter or the rim of a glass to cut out scones.
Gather the scraps, re-form the dough, and cut out additional scones.
Place the scones on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space between each scone.
Brush the tops of the scones with melted butter.
Bake the scones in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until they are golden brown.
